title: Task Queue
description: The you-agent-factory task queue is the backlog of submitted work waiting in work-type states until workstations consume it.
openingSummary: A task queue is the factory backlog of submitted work sitting in work-type states and places until enabled workstations can consume ready items.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: A task queue is the you-agent-factory backlog of submitted work. After work enters the factory, it becomes tokens waiting in work-type states and places until workstations can consume ready items.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: The task queue keeps many work items persistent after submit so a long-running factory can dispatch them in parallel or in order as workstations become enabled.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: You submit a work item. It lands in an initial work-type state such as task:init. A workstation whose inputs match that waiting place becomes enabled and consumes the item.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: The task queue is the runtime backlog of waiting work, not a planner checklist, not the submit batch that enqueues work, not the token vocabulary itself, and not the workqueue-executor technique pattern.
